question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user send repeated AccountBalance requests omitting BlockIdentifier so that RetrieveLatest() is called on every request, causing the blockRepo to execute a DB query each time, and sustaining elevated CPU and DB I/O that increases node resource consumption by at least 30% compared to the preceding 24 hours?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user craft an AccountBalanceRequest with a BlockIdentifier containing only a Hash (no Index) so that RetrieveBlock() calls blockRepo.FindByHash(), triggering a full hash-based DB scan on every request, causing sustained elevated DB resource consumption exceeding 30% above baseline?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user craft an AccountBalanceRequest with a BlockIdentifier containing only an Index (no Hash) so that RetrieveBlock() calls blockRepo.FindByIndex(), and by cycling through many distinct block indices in sequential requests, force repeated expensive index-based DB lookups that increase node resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user craft an AccountBalanceRequest with both Hash and Index set in BlockIdentifier so that RetrieveBlock() calls blockRepo.FindByIdentifier() with both parameters, triggering a compound DB query, and by sending many such requests with distinct valid block identifiers, increase node resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a hex-encoded alias address (0x-prefixed) as AccountIdentifier.Address so that NewAccountIdFromString() triggers hex.DecodeString() and NewAccountIdFromAlias() including public key parsing on every request, causing elevated CPU usage from repeated cryptographic operations that increases node resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a maximally long valid hex-encoded alias string as AccountIdentifier.Address so that NewAccountIdFromString() allocates a large byte slice via hex.DecodeString() and passes it to NewAccountIdFromAlias() on every request, causing memory allocation pressure that increases node resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user send AccountBalance requests targeting very old block indices (low ConsensusEndNanos) so that accountRepo.RetrieveBalanceAtBlock() must scan a large historical range of balance snapshots and balance changes, causing expensive DB range scans that increase node resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user send AccountBalance requests targeting the genesis block so that accountRepo.RetrieveBalanceAtBlock() is forced to compute balance from the earliest possible ConsensusEndNanos, requiring the largest possible historical DB scan, increasing node resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: AllAccountBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user call AllAccountBalances() instead of AccountBalance() so that the service internally constructs a new AccountBalanceRequest and calls AccountBalance() as a delegate, doubling the per-request processing overhead and increasing node resource consumption by at least 30% compared to direct AccountBalance() calls?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: AllAccountBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user send AllAccountBalances requests with no BlockIdentifier so that both AllAccountBalances() and the delegated AccountBalance() each trigger RetrieveLatest() calls, causing two blockRepo DB queries per request and increasing node resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: AllAccountBalances()] Can an unprivileged external user craft AllAccountBalancesRequest with a BlockIdentifier containing only a Hash so that AllAccountBalances() delegates to AccountBalance() which calls blockRepo.FindByHash(), and by sending many such requests with distinct hashes, cause sustained hash-based DB scans that increase node resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user send AccountBalance requests with a BlockIdentifier whose Hash field contains a very long hex string (with 0x prefix) so that tools.SafeRemoveHexPrefix() and blockRepo.FindByHash() process an oversized string on every request, causing string allocation and DB query overhead that increases node resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user send AccountBalance requests for many distinct valid account IDs (shard.realm.num format) each targeting a different historical block, so that accountRepo.RetrieveBalanceAtBlock() executes a unique DB query per account-block pair, causing a high volume of distinct DB queries that increases node resource consumption by at least 30%?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/account_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Increasing network processing node resource consumption by at least 30% without brute force actions, compared to the preceding 24 hours'] [Function: AccountBalance()] Can an unprivileged external user send AccountBalance requests for accounts with alias addresses that resolve to accounts with large token portfolios, so that accountRepo.RetrieveBalanceAtBlock() returns a large AmountSlice requiring significant serialization work in balances.ToRosetta(), increasing CPU and memory consumption by at least 30%?
